perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees that it wil l take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the highest degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.changed_text: Beneficiary agrees that it wil l take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the industry's degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'all reasonable measures' to 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'highest degree of care' to 'industry's degree of care' introduces a contradiction. The first change lowers the standard of care expected from the Recipient, while the second change ties the standard of care to the industry standard. Since it is still reffering to Recipient in the statement, it creates in-text contradiction, since Recipient doesn't exist anymore, and the term for that role is Beneficiary, as described in the first change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non -Disclosure of Confidential Information. The Recipient agrees not to use the Confidential Information for any purpose other than that s et forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. The Recipient will not disclose any Confidential Information to third parties except those directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient who are required to have the information in order to car ry out the purpose set forth in Section 1 of this Agreement. Recipient has had or will have directors, officers, employees, consultants and agents of Recipient to whom Confidential Information is disclosed or who have access to Confidential Information sig n a Non -Disclosure Agreement in content substantially similar to this Agreement and will promptly notify the Discloser in writing of the names of each such person who has signed such agreements after such agreements are signed. Beneficiary agrees that it wil l take commercially reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of Confidential Information in order to prevent it from falling into the public domain or the possession of persons other than those persons authorized hereunder to have a ny such information, which measures shall include the industry's degree of care that Recipient utilizes to protect its own Confidential Information of a similar nature. Recipient agrees to notify the Discloser in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of s uch Confidential Information which may come to its attention. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA )
perturbation.justification: The change from 'all reasonable measures' to 'commercially reasonable measures' and 'highest degree of care' to 'industry's degree of care' lowers the standard of care expected, creates an in-text contradiction. The original text obligates the 'Recipient', but the changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'Beneficiary', creating a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text' which still refers to the role as 'Recipient', as it doesn't exist in the changed text section anymore, causing confusion and unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Mandatory Disclosure. In the event that the Recipient or its directors, officers, employees, consultants or agents are requested or required by legal process to disclose any of the Confident ial Information, the Recipient shall give prompt notice so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate relief.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Compulsory Disclosure. In the event that the Beneficiary or its directors, officers, employees, consultants or agents are requested or required by legal process to disclose any of the Proprietary Information, the Beneficiary shall give prompt notice so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate relief.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Mandatory Disclosure' to 'Compulsory Disclosure' and 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' creates an in-text contradiction because the term 'Proprietary Information' is not defined anywhere in the document, while 'Confidential Information' is. The text is now contradicting itself because it refers to a previously named agent (Beneficiary), after changing it in the first perturbation. The term has not been defined, but it is being reffered by name.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Compulsory Disclosure. In the event that the Beneficiary or its directors, officers, employees, consultants or agents are requested or required by legal process to disclose any of the Proprietary Information, the Beneficiary shall give prompt notice so that the Discloser may seek a protective order or other appropriate relief. In the event that such protective order is not obtained, the Recipient shall disclose only that portion of the Confidenti al Information which its counsel advises that it is legally required to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information', which is not defined in the document, while the contradicted text refers to both 'Proprietary Information' and 'Confidential Information'. The change thus creates a contradiction by using an undefined term where a defined term was previously used, creating uncertainty about the scope of the disclosure obligation. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology, which leads to in-text contradictions when the contract uses terms interchangeably without defining them.
